code:
from dataclasses import dataclass

@dataclass
class Triple:
    subject: str
    predicate: str
    object: str

# EVOLVE-BLOCK-START

def predict(triples: list[Triple]) -> str:
    """Convert a list of triples about an athlete (or related entities) into a fluent, combined sentence."""
    if not triples:
        return ""

    # Templates that return a fragment *without* the subject.
    fragment_templates = {
        "birthDate": lambda o: f"was born on {o}",
        "birthYear": lambda o: f"was born in {o}",
        "birthPlace": lambda o: f"was born in {o}",
        "height": lambda o: f"is {o} meters tall",
        "weight": lambda o: f"weighs {o}",
        "position": lambda o: f"plays as a {o}",
        "club": lambda o: f"plays for {o}",
        "currentclub": lambda o: f"plays for {o}",
        "formerTeam": lambda o: f"formerly played for {o}",
        "league": lambda o: f"competes in the {o}",
        "manager": lambda o: f"is managed by {o}",
        "coach": lambda o: f"is coached by {o}",
        "draftYear": lambda o: f"was drafted in {o}",
        "draftRound": lambda o: f"was selected in round {o}",
        "draftPick": lambda o: f"was the {o}th pick",
        "playerNumber": lambda o: f"wears number {o}",
        "currentteam": lambda o: f"currently plays for {o}",
        "debutTeam": lambda o: f"debuted with {o}",
        "leader": lambda o: f"is led by {o}",
        "deathPlace": lambda o: f"died in {o}",
        "isPartOf": lambda o: f"is part of {o}",
        "country": lambda o: f"is in {o}",
        "city": lambda o: f"is located in {o}",
        "ground": lambda o: f"has home ground {o}",
        "stadium": lambda o: f"has stadium {o}",
        "owner": lambda o: f"is owned by {o}",
        "generalManager": lambda o: f"has general manager {o}",
        "chairman": lambda o: f"has chairman {o}",
        "season": lambda o: f"participated in the {o} season",
        "currency": lambda o: f"uses {o} as currency",
        "demonym": lambda o: f"has demonym {o}",
        "officialLanguage": lambda o: f"has official language {o}",
        "language": lambda o: f"has language {o}",
        "ethnicGroup": lambda o: f"has ethnic group {o}",
        "anthem": lambda o: f"has anthem {o}",
        "mostChampions": lambda o: f"has most champions {o}",
        "leaderTitle": lambda o: f"has leader title {o}",
        "utcOffset": lambda o: f"has UTC offset {o}",
        "timeZone": lambda o: f"is in time zone {o}",
        "areaTotal": lambda o: f"covers an area of {o}",
        "foundingDate": lambda o: f"was founded on {o}",
    }

    # Group fragments by subject to enable combined sentences.
    subject_map: dict[str, list[str]] = {}
    for t in triples:
        s, p, o = t.subject, t.predicate, t.object
        frag_func = fragment_templates.get(p)
        if frag_func:
            fragment = frag_func(o)
        else:
            fragment = f"has {p} {o}"
        subject_map.setdefault(s, []).append(fragment)

    sentences = []
    for subject, fragments in subject_map.items():
        if not fragments:
            continue
        if len(fragments) == 1:
            sentence = f"{subject} {fragments[0]}."
        else:
            # Join fragments with commas and an 'and' before the last one.
            body = ", ".join(fragments[:-1]) + ", and " + fragments[-1]
            sentence = f"{subject} {body}."
        sentences.append(sentence)

    # Combine multiple subject sentences into one paragraph-like output.
    if not sentences:
        return ""
    if len(sentences) == 1:
        return sentences[0]
    return " ".join(sentences)

# EVOLVE-BLOCK-END
